name: datatrails-scitt-samples
documentation:
![Logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datatrails/datatrails-scitt-samples/main/DataTrails_Horizontal_Logo_Black.png#gh-light-mode-only)
![Logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datatrails/datatrails-scitt-samples/main/DataTrails_Horizontal_Logo_White.png#gh-dark-mode-only)

# DataTrails SCITT Examples

The files in this repository can be used to demonstrate how the DataTrails SCITT API works.

For usage, please see [Quickstart: SCITT Statements (Preview)](https://docs.datatrails.ai/developers/developer-patterns/scitt-api/)

The python package produced by this repository is tested to a production standard.

Its purpose is to provide a clear example of how to use the current DataTrails SCITT API's,
as such, no promise of backwards compatibility is given.

Developers are encouraged to use the sources in this repositor as reference
material for their own integrations.

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.scripts.datatrails_event_info
documentation:
The DataTrails transparencey service embeds the signed statement in a DataTrails event.

This is an example of how to pick out and introspect the signed statement
directly using the proprietary DataTrails API.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.scripts.datatrails_event_info.main
documentation:
Reports information about an event

The event can come from a file on disc or be fetched from the DataTrails service.

If no authorization is provided, the event is assumed to be available on the public endpoint.

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.apitoken
documentation:
Resolve a client id and secret to a DataTrails API authorization token.

Registering a statement on the Data Trails transparency ledger requires an API token.

documentation:
Get DataTrails bearer token. If a configuration is not provided, it will be
loaded from the environment.

documentation: Convert a SCITT Entry ID to a DataTrails Event Identity

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.scripts.register_signed_statement
documentation:
Module for submitting a SCITT signed statement to the
DataTrails Transparency Service and optionally returning
a Transparent Statement

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.scripts.register_signed_statement.attach_receipt
documentation:
Given a Signed Statement file on disc and the provided receipt content, from
the Transparency Service, read the statement fromm disc, attach the provided
receipt, writing the re-encoded result back to disc.  The resulting
re-encoded statement is now a Transparent Statement.

The caller is expected to have *verified* the receipt first.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.scripts.register_signed_statement.main
documentation: Creates a Transparent Statement

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.servicecontext
documentation:
Provides a minimal context for various DataTrails service interactions.

Notably:
* Authentication & Authorization
* Logging
* Development override of the service url

classes.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.servicecontext.ServiceContext

methods.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.servicecontext.ServiceContext.from_env
documentation:
Create a service context from the environment.

With optional overrides given precedence.

Args:
    require_auth:
        If True, the environment must be configured with
        DATATRAILS_CLIENT_ID and DATATRAILS_CLIENT_SECRET.
        If these are provided on the commandline, set them in cfg_overrides
        and set require_auth=False.

    cfg_overrides:
        can be any of the fields defined on ServiceConfig. This allows
        precedence to be given to commandline arguments.

methods.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.servicecontext.ServiceContext.from_config
documentation:
Create a service context from a configuration object

Initialize the logger with the provided configuration.

documentation: error logging convenience method
Convenience defaults for the logging methods

methods.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.servicecontext.ServiceContext.info
documentation: info logging convenience method

methods.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.servicecontext.ServiceContext.debug
documentation: debug logging convenience method

Defines a context for interacting with the DataTrails service.

Automatically obtains the configuration from the environment, allowing for
imperative overrides

Example use:

    args = parser.parse_args()
    cfg_overrides = {}
    if args.datatrails_url:
        cfg_overrides["datatrails_url"] = args.datatrails_url
    ctx = ServiceContext.from_env("register-statement", **cfg_overrides)

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.v3eventhash
documentation:
This module illustrates how to calculate the append only log Merkle leaf hash
of a scitt statement registered on the Data Trails transparency ledger.

Currently the DataTrails implementation, scitt statements are recorded as a
base64 encoded event attribute. To reproduce the leaf hash from appendn only
log, this original
[event](https://docs.datatrails.ai/platform/overview/core-concepts/#events) data
is required to obtain the hash.

This module implements the full process for obtaining the event and generating
the ledger leaf hash.

See KB:

https://support.datatrails.ai/hc/en-gb/articles/18120936244370-How-to-independently-verify-Merkle-Log-Events-recorded-on-the-DataTrails-transparency-ledger#h_01HTYDD6ZH0FV2K95D61RQ61ZJ

This limitation will be removed in a future release of the DataTrails API.

Note that if you have access to the DataTrails UI, the leaf hash will match what
is displayed there for the public view of the event.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.v3eventhash.v3leaf_hash
documentation:
Return the leaf hash which is proven by a scitt receipt for the provided CONFIRMED event

Computes:

SHA256(BYTE(0x00) || BYTES(idTimestamp) || BENCODE(redactedEvent))

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.v3eventhash.v3event_hash
documentation: Returns the V3 event hash

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.v3eventhash.get_mmrsalt
documentation:
Get the public salt details from a v3 event record.

Returns the bytes comprised of

DOMAIN || BYTES(IDTIMESTAMP)

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.v3eventhash.get_v3preimage
documentation: Calculate the leaf hash of a V3 leaf

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples
documentation: Archivist SCITT Samples

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.eventpreimage
documentation:
Obtain the merkle log leaf hash and event hash for a DataTrails event

For SCITT Statements registered with datatrails, the leaf hash currently
includes content that is additional to the signed statement.  It currently
requires a proprietary API call to DataTrails to obtain that content.  The
content is available on a public access endpoint (no authorisation is required)

These limitations are not inherent to the SCITT architecture.  The are specific
to the current DataTrails implementation, and will be addressed in future
releases.

Note that the leaf hash can be read directly from the merkle log given only
information in the receipt.  And, as the log data is public and easily
replicable, this does not require interaction with datatrails.

However, on its own, this does not show that the leaf hash commits the statement
to the log.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.eventpreimage.get_leaf_hash
documentation:
Obtain the leaf hash for a given event identity

The leaf hash is the value that is proven by the COSE Receipt attached to
the transparent statement.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.eventpreimage.get_signed_statement
documentation:
Obtain the signed statement for a given event identity

The signed statement is the value that is registered with the DataTrails
service.  It is the value that is signed by the statement counter signing
key.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.eventpreimage.get_event
documentation: Fetch the event from the DataTrails service event api

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.datatrails.eventpreimage.signed_statement_from_event
documentation: Extract the signed statement from an event

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_registration
documentation:
SCITT Statement Registration

This module provides functions to register a signed statement with the DataTrails

# pylint: disable=line-too-long
Per https://ietf-wg-scitt.github.io/draft-ietf-scitt-architecture/draft-ietf-scitt-architecture.html#name-registration

They are defined in the expected order of use

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_registration.submit_statement
documentation:
Given a Signed Statement CBOR file on disk, register it on the DataTrails
Transparency Service over the SCITT interface

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_registration.submit_statement_from_file
documentation:
Given a Signed Statement CBOR file on disk, register it on the DataTrails
Transparency Service over the SCITT interface

documentation: Gets the status of a long-running registration operation

documentation: Polls for the operation status to be 'succeeded'.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_registration.get_receipt
documentation: Get the receipt for the provided entry id

Includes a workaround for a bug in the common datatrails cose library.

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.cose_sign1message
documentation:
Handling for COSE_Sign1 messages

Specific accomodation for detached payloads.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.cose_sign1message.extract_to_be_signed
documentation:
Get the bytes that need to be signed for remote signing

When using a Sign1Message this way, If the configured algorithm does not
match the remote sign operation, verification will fail.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.cose_sign1message.decode_sign1_detached
documentation:
Decodes a COSE sign1 message from a message with a detached payload.

For COSE Receipts the caller can not provide payload in advance.
The payload is dependent on the receipt's unprotected header contents which are only available
after calling this function.

WARNING: The message will NOT VERIFY unless the payload is replaced with the
payload that was signed.

Args:
    message: the bytes of the COSE sign1 message
    payload:
        Used as the payload if not none, otherwise payload is forced to b''.
        Verification will fail until the correct payload has been set on the returned
        Sign1Message.

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.cose_receipt_verification
documentation: Verification of the MMRIVER draft-bryce-cose-merkle-mountain-range-proofs receipt

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.cose_receipt_verification.verify_receipt_mmriver
documentation:
Verifies the counter signed receipt signature
Args:
    receipt: COSE Receipt as cbor encoded bytes
    leaf: append only log leaf hash proven by the receipt. provided as bytes

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation
documentation:
The issuer creates the statement and signs it.

The statement will then be registered with one or more transparency services.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.create_hashed_signed_statement
documentation:
creates a hashed signed statement, given the signing_key, payload, subject and issuer
the payload will be hashed and the hash added to the payload field.
pylint: disable=too-many-positional-arguments

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.create_hashed_statement
documentation:
creates a hashed signed statement, given the verification_key, payload, subject and issuer
the payload will be hashed and the hash added to the payload field.

For remote signing, use cose_sign1message.extract_to_be_signed() to get the bytes that need to be signed.

Further alg  & curve support can be added as needed.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.create_signed_statement
documentation: creates a signed statement, given the signing_key, payload, subject and issuer
pylint: disable=too-many-positional-arguments

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.create_statement
documentation:
creates a statement, given the verification_key, payload, subject and issuer.

For remote signing, use cose_sign1message.extract_to_be_signed() to get the bytes that need to be signed.

Further alg  & curve support can be added as needed.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.hashed_payload_protected_header
documentation: Populate the SCITT protected header basics for a hashed payload.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.inline_payload_protected_header
documentation: Populate the SCITT protected header basics for a hashed payload.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.protected_header_cwt
documentation:
Create the HEADER_LABEL_CWT value for the protected header.

Typically used when remote signing to communicate the verification key to the statement consumer.

The result of this function can be used to populate protected_header[HEADER_LABEL_CWT].

The provided alg should also be set in the protected header top level label
HEADER_LABEL_ALGORITHM.

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.verifying_key_header_ec2_p256
documentation:
Create the HEADER_LABEL_CNF_COSE_KEY value for the protected header.

When remote signing with the EC2 algo on the P256 curve.

The result of this function can be used to populate

protected_header[HEADER_LABEL_CWT][HEADER_LABEL_CWT_CNF][HEADER_LABEL_CNF_COSE_KEY]

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.statement_creation.cose_key_ec2_p256
documentation: Create a cose_key instance for locally signing a statement.

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.mmriver.decodeinclusionproof
documentation:
Support for decoding inclusion proofs defined by the MMRIVER specification.

https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-bryce-cose-merkle-mountain-range-proofs-00.html

Which are a VDS tree algorithm for COSE receipts, which is defined by
https://cose-wg.github.io/draft-ietf-cose-merkle-tree-proofs/draft-ietf-cose-merkle-tree-proofs.html

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.mmriver.decodeinclusionproof.decode_inclusion_proofs
documentation:
COSE Receipts
Checks the headers of the mmriver receipt for the correct values
and returns a list of inclusion proofs.

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.dump_cbor
documentation: Module for dumping a CBOR file

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.dump_cbor.main
documentation: Dumps content of a supposed CBOR file

modules.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.mmriver.algorithms
documentation:
Selective copy of

https://github.com/robinbryce/draft-bryce-cose-merkle-mountain-range-proofs/blob/main/algorithms.py

Which is a reference implementation of

https://robinbryce.github.io/draft-bryce-cose-merkle-mountain-range-proofs/draft-bryce-cose-merkle-mountain-range-proofs.html

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.mmriver.algorithms.included_root
documentation:
Apply the proof to nodehash to produce the implied root

For a valid cose receipt of inclusion, using the returned root as the
detached payload will result in a receipt message whose signature can be
verified.

Args:
    i (int): the mmr index where `nodehash` is located.
    nodehash (bytes): the value whose inclusion is being proven.
    proof (List[bytes]): the siblings required to produce `root` from `nodehash`.

Returns:
    the root hash produced for `nodehash` using `path`

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.mmriver.algorithms.index_height
documentation: Returns the 0 based height of the mmr entry indexed by i

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.mmriver.algorithms.hash_pospair64
documentation:
Compute the hash of  pos || a || b

Args:
    pos (int): the 1-based position of an mmr node. If a, b are left and
        right children, pos should be the parent position.
    a (bytes): the first value to include in the hash
    b (bytes): the second value to include in the hash

Returns:
    The value for the node identified by pos

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.mmriver.algorithms.most_sig_bit
documentation: Returns the mask for the the most significant bit in pos

functions.name: datatrails_scitt_samples.mmriver.algorithms.all_ones
documentation: Returns true if all bits, starting with the most significant, are 1
